cheatsheet.title: Converting Iterator Loops to Enhanced For Loops

intro.description: This cheat sheet guides you through using the Iterator to For Loop cleanup to replace verbose Iterator-based while loops with concise enhanced for loops (for-each).

item.title: Understand the Transformation
Enable the Cleanup
item.description:
Open the Clean Up dialog to enable the Iterator conversion cleanup:
1. In the Package Explorer, select a Java project
2. Choose Source → Clean Up…
3. Select Use custom profile and click Configure…
4. On the Code Style or Sandbox Tools tab, enable Convert Iterator loops to enhanced for loops
5. Click OK
Click Perform to open the Source menu.

item.title: Run the Cleanup on Java Source Files
item.description:
Apply the cleanup to your Java source files:
1. Open or select Java files that contain Iterator-based while loops
2. Choose Source → Clean Up…
3. Confirm the profile has the Iterator conversion option enabled
4. Click Finish
All eligible Iterator-based loops will be replaced with enhanced for loops.

item.title: Review the Simplified Loop Code
item.description:
After the cleanup runs, verify the transformed loops:
• Iterator declarations and hasNext() / next() calls are removed
• The loop variable is declared directly in the for-each header
• The loop body remains unchanged
If a loop used it.remove() or the Iterator variable after the loop, it is left unchanged since the transformation would alter semantics.
Run your tests to verify correctness after the cleanup.
